_comment_tier1: These dominate runtime vs. quality. Sweep these first (cheapest to vary).

max_cg_iterations._comment: CG iterations per B&B node. Low values risk a loose LP bound; high values overspend on near-root nodes. 50-100 covers most VRPP instances well.

max_routes_per_pricing._comment: Columns added per pricing call. More columns = richer LP signal, but larger master LP. Beyond ~50 the master LP solve time dominates. Start with 20.

max_cuts_per_iteration._comment: Cuts added per separation round. Too few = loose LP; too many = dense master. 5-10 is the empirical sweet spot for VRPP.

max_cut_iterations._comment: Separation rounds per CG convergence. More rounds tighten the LP but delay return to pricing. Combined with max_cuts_per_iteration.

ng_neighborhood_size._comment: Size of ng-route neighborhoods. Larger = tighter relaxation but exponential label growth. 8 is the practical standard; 12 is near-exact for VRPP at 100 nodes. Combined with DSSR.

optimality_gap._comment: Relative B&B termination gap. 0.005 (0.5%) is near-optimal for VRPP within 300s. Tightening below 0.001 rarely changes the solution but can double runtime.

early_termination_gap._comment: Heuristic early stop: accepted sub-optimality once a good incumbent is found. Must be >= optimality_gap. Values above 0.05 risk missing near-optimal solutions.

rc_tolerance._comment: Minimum reduced cost to accept a new column. Too loose (>optimality_gap) triggers correctness warning in bpc_engine. Keep well below optimality_gap.

cut_orthogonality_threshold._comment: Cosine-similarity ceiling for cut filtering. Lower = more diverse cuts per round (tighter LP faster) but higher filter overhead. 0.7-0.8 is the empirical sweet spot.

_comment_tier2: Each flag independently enables a mechanism. Sweep these after Tier 1.

cg_at_root_only._comment: CRITICAL correctness lever. False = re-price at every B&B node (mandatory for near-optimality). True = heuristic bounds at non-root nodes, may prune incorrectly. Only set True to benchmark root-only vs. full CG speed tradeoff.

enable_node_visitation_branching._comment: Hierarchical y_v branching before arc/RF branching (Boussier et al. 2007). Directly resolves VRPP profit-selection x routing duality. Should be True for VRPP; disabling is useful as a baseline only.

enable_dssr._comment: Decremental State-Space Relaxation (Righini & Salani 2008). Iteratively tightens ng-route relaxation toward elementarity. Decisive for VRPP (Keshtkaran et al. 2016). Disable only to isolate ng-route vs. DSSR contribution.

enable_reduced_cost_arc_fixing._comment: Eliminates arcs from pricing graph when their min reduced cost exceeds the LP-incumbent gap (Irnich et al. 2010). Typically removes 20-40% of arcs in later B&B nodes. Almost always beneficial.

lr_pre_pruning._comment: Lagrangian pre-pruning via subgradient before CG at each B&B node. Beneficial at >100 nodes within 300s budget. Less impactful at the root (strong LP already). Pairs with lr_warm_start_cg.

lr_warm_start_cg._comment: Seed CG column pool with the UOP solution at lambda* from LR. Only meaningful when lr_pre_pruning=True. Provides a stronger CG starting point at the cost of one extra OP solve per node.

enable_strong_branching_heuristic._comment: Phase 1/2 LP evaluation of top branching candidates. Reduces B&B node count by 20-40% but costs ~0.5s per candidate LP solve. Beneficial when time_limit >= 120s and max_bb_nodes is large.

profit_aware_operators._comment: Profit-aware greedy insertion for warm-start routes. Ensures optional nodes have non-zero LP duals from iteration 1, guiding RCSPP toward profitable subsets immediately.

enable_column_pool_deduplication._comment: Periodically removes duplicate columns from the master LP. Keeps master LP tractable over long B&B runs. Almost always beneficial at negligible cost.

_comment_tier3: These interact with Tier 1 numerics. Sweep only after Tier 1 and Tier 2 are tuned.

max_bb_nodes._comment: Global B&B node limit. At 300s with DFS, 1000-2000 nodes is a practical ceiling before time_limit fires first. Higher values matter only when individual nodes are fast (<0.1s).

rcspp_timeout._comment: Per-pricing-call time cap. At 300s total and cg_at_root_only=False, 10-15s is appropriate. Heuristic pricing always runs first (<0.1s); exact DP fires only if heuristic is insufficient.

rcspp_max_labels._comment: Max label count in RCSPP DP. Python-speed labeling (~10k labels/s) means the rcspp_timeout fires before this cap at 100 nodes. Exists as an OOM safety guard; sweep only if label density is unexpectedly high.

strong_branching_size._comment: Candidates evaluated in Phase 1 strong branching. Only active when enable_strong_branching_heuristic=True. Beyond 5 the marginal node-count reduction rarely justifies the LP solve overhead.

dssr_max_iters._comment: DSSR refinement iterations per pricing call. Convergence typically happens in 2-4 iterations; 8 is a safe ceiling. Only active when enable_dssr=True.

_comment_tier4: Only sweep when lr_pre_pruning=True is confirmed beneficial in Tier 2.

lr_max_subgradient_iters._comment: Polyak iterations per B&B node in the LR subgradient pass. Keep low (20-40): runs at every node. Diminishing returns beyond 40 for warm-started multipliers.

lr_op_time_limit._comment: Wall-clock budget per uncapacitated OP solve inside the LR subgradient. The engine caps total LR budget at 5% of remaining wall time regardless of this value.

lr_subgradient_theta._comment: Polyak step-size multiplier theta in (0, 2]. Values > 1.5 can cause oscillation near optimality. 1.0 is the standard safe default.

lr_pre_pruning_depth_limit._comment: -1 = apply LR pruning at all depths. 0 = root only. Positive integers restrict to shallow nodes where the LR bound is strongest. Sweeping this isolates where LR earns its overhead.

_comment_fixed: These are pinned by correctness constraints or structural requirements. The single choice is applied verbatim to every trial.

search_strategy._comment: Fixed: DFS is required for LP basis reuse across parent->child transitions (3-5x speedup on child LP solves). Best-first or BFS would invalidate warm-starting.

branching_strategy._comment: Fixed: spatial divergence branching (Barnhart et al. 1998) is structurally required for prefer_shorter_path_dfs and node_visitation_branching to function correctly.

cutting_planes._comment: Fixed: for near-optimal VRPP the composite cut suite (RCC + SRI + Min-Cut + Triangle Clique + limited-memory rank-1 + NodeProfit + PathElimination) is always superior. Sweeping against rcc-only is an ablation study, not quality optimisation.

prefer_shorter_path_dfs._comment: Fixed: correctness-adjacent setting matching paper §3.2 child ordering. Uniformly beneficial when branching_strategy=divergence.

enable_heuristic_rcc_separation._comment: Fixed: max-flow RCC separation is cheap and finds violated cuts the connected-component heuristic misses. Disabling consistently weakens the LP bound.

enable_comb_cuts._comment: Fixed: comb separation is O(n^3) per round — ~5s per call at 100 nodes. Min-Cut and Triangle Clique provide better gap closure per second for VRPP.

use_spatial_partitioning._comment: Fixed: the flow-sorted median split is already well-balanced. Coordinate-based partitioning adds overhead without measurable benefit on tested instances.

enable_hybrid_search._comment: Fixed: pure DFS is superior to hybrid DFS/BFS when cg_at_root_only=False because basis reuse requires visiting child nodes immediately after their parent.

exact_mode._comment: Fixed: Wentges dual smoothing (exact_mode=False) is 2-5x faster in practice. Disabling it (exact_mode=True) is theoretically pure but CG convergence degrades severely within any finite time budget.

vrpp._comment: Fixed: problem type flag, not a hyperparameter.

lr_lambda_init._comment: Fixed: starting the subgradient from the unpenalised point is universally robust. Non-zero warm-starts risk bias at early nodes where no incumbent yet exists.

route_budget._comment: Fixed: set to infinity (largest finite float). Enables Triangle Clique and Path Elimination cuts; without a finite travel-time bound these cuts reduce to no-ops. Override if vehicle time-window constraints are known.
